what_it_does: Defines a React Gallery screen with a title, action toolbar, selectable image tiles, a grid/list view toggle control, and a video tile section with play indicators, then exposes the Gallery component on window.
observations: Uses React useState aliased as useState_g.
MediaTile renders an empty visual tile whose selected and video states are expressed through CSS classes.
Image selection is stored as a Set initialized with indices 0, 6, and 8.
The view state switches button active styling between grid and list but the rendered tile layout remains cv-tile-grid.
Images and videos are generated from fixed-length arrays rather than data objects.
Inline SVGs are used for play, grid, and list icons.
Gallery assigns itself to window.Gallery instead of using an ES module export.
standouts: The screen is a static gallery mockup driven by local UI state and fixed tile counts.
Selection behavior exists for image tiles but not for video tiles.
The list/grid toggle is present as UI state without conditional list rendering in this file.
conventions: Uses global React and global component references rather than local imports.
Uses cv-* class naming for styling hooks.
Uses inline style objects for small layout adjustments.
Uses window assignment for component exposure.
concerns: Clickable MediaTile uses a div without keyboard handling, role, or tabIndex.
The list view state changes only button styling and does not alter the tile layout.
ActionToolbar is referenced without a local import or definition.
Generated media tiles have no labels, image sources, ids, or metadata in this file.
View toggle buttons do not specify type="button".
notes: This file is a gallery screen stub focused on visual layout, selection state, and view-control affordances.
questions: Should list view render a different layout or is the toggle only decorative here?
Should video tiles support selection or click behavior like image tiles?
Where are tile visuals, media sources, and ActionToolbar supplied from?
Should MediaTile be keyboard-accessible as an interactive element?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename and screen title indicate a Gallery screen, and the file implements a gallery-style UI with image and video sections; some controls are present as affordances without full behavior in this file.
intention_signals: It seems intended to demonstrate a gallery screen pattern with selectable media tiles, basic view switching controls, and separated image/video sections within the platform UI kit.
novelty: The component exposes a design-system gallery mockup through globals while using local state to simulate selection and view mode without external data.
contribution: Contributes a compact gallery substrate: selectable media tile visuals, image and video section structure, toolbar placement, view-toggle affordance, and fixed mock content useful for a fused system's media-browsing surface.
summary_for_embedding: A React Gallery screen prototype rendering a toolbar, selectable image tile grid, grid/list toggle controls, and video tiles with play overlays using local state and cv-* styling hooks.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for presenting a gallery UI pattern in a design-system/platform kit, showing media selection and browsing affordances without wired data loading.

symbols.does: Renders a gallery tile with optional selected styling, optional video styling, optional play overlay, and an onClick handler.
symbols.description: MediaTile is a presentational component whose visible state is driven by selected and video props. It does not render media content itself; instead it supplies class names and an inline SVG play indicator for video tiles.

symbols.does: Renders the Gallery screen with toolbar controls, image tiles, a view toggle, and a video section.
symbols.description: Gallery owns the local selected-tile Set and grid/list view state. It creates fixed image and video tile collections, wires image tile clicks to selection toggling, and assigns the component to window.Gallery for global access.

symbols.does: Toggles an image index in the selected Set and updates selection state.
symbols.description: toggle copies the current Set, removes the index if present, adds it otherwise, and stores the new Set with setSel. It is used by image MediaTile onClick handlers to switch selected styling per tile.
